page: Microsoft Azure E96-48ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance belonging to the Edsv6 family. Powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 24 physical cores and 48 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 5669 GB, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and processing capabilities. The instance achieves a strong memory bandwidth read-only small block speed of 3723.24 GB/sec and a large block read-only speed of 234.94 GB/sec, though large block write bandwidth is average at 84.9 GB/sec. CPU performance is highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2149.0 and a multi-core score of 18862.0, both categorized as strong. Image processing performance is top-tier, scoring 19373.0. In database operations, the server demonstrates strong capabilities with a PassMark database score of 24034.71 and Redis SET throughput reaching 67.4 million operations per second.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8). These hardware extensions support workloads like LLM inference, where the server achieves a strong prompt processing speed of 27.41 tokens/second on a large 70B model, though text generation for the same model drops to an average of 3.28 tokens/second. Given the high memory density, substantial local storage, and strong database performance, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database management, large-scale data processing, and software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the Edsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of RAM and 5669 GB of local storage. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmark results show strong performance across memory bandwidth, with small block read speeds of 3723.24 GB/sec, and database operations, with a PassMark database score of 24034.71 and 67.4 million Redis SET operations per second. Although single-core and multi-core CPU performance is strong, large block write memory bandwidth remains average at 84.9 GB/sec. This high-density memory configuration, combined with substantial local storage, makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48ds_v6 is a memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high-capacity local storage, advanced matrix extensions, and strong database and memory bandwidth performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores at 4.2 GHz
768.0 GB of RAM providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core
5669 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory read bandwidth and database performance benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Best-fit for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, and data processing
